evals.name: dev-rfc-review-ui
evals.prompt: I've got this skill at skills/.experimental/dev-rfc/ that generates an HTML review page for RFC documents. The review template at assets/review_template.html renders user-submitted RFC markdown and displays it in the browser. Can you do a security analysis of this skill? I want to understand what could go wrong.
evals.expected_output: A structured threat model that identifies the XSS vulnerability in review_template.html (marked.parse rendering untrusted markdown into innerHTML without DOMPurify), classifies RFC markdown as attacker-controlled input, and calibrates severity considering it runs on localhost.

evals.name: threat-patch-self-analysis
evals.prompt: Can you threat model the skill at skills/.experimental/threat-patch/? It parses Codex security finding CSVs and generates code patches. I'm worried about what happens if someone feeds it a malicious CSV or if the patch workflow has unintended side effects.
evals.expected_output: A threat model that identifies: the parse-findings.sh script processes untrusted CSV input, the skill modifies source code (write risk), config.json has path fields, and hooks.json gates some but not all destructive operations.

evals.name: harness-scripts-low-surface
evals.prompt: Run a threat model on the build-agents-md.js and validate-skill.js scripts. They're Node.js build/validation tools that read skill directories and check structure. These are internal tools. I just want a quick check that they're not doing anything dangerous.
evals.expected_output: A proportionate, brief threat model. These are read-only validation scripts processing trusted developer input. Should identify low risk and avoid inflating non-threats.
